example: Do you want to hang out this weekend?
note: 친구와 편하게 시간 보낼 때 가장 흔한 표현

example: Let's grab a bite before the movie.
example_ko: 영화 보기 전에 간단히 뭐 좀 먹자.
note: grab a coffee(커피 한잔하다)도 같은 패턴

example: Pizza tonight? — I'm down!
example_ko: 오늘 밤 피자 어때? — 완전 좋지!

example: Don't worry about it. It's no big deal.
I'm kind of tired today.
Hey, what's up? Long time no see!
note: 인사로 쓰면 대답은 Not much가 무난

example: Hold on, I'll be right back.
Sorry, I'm running late. Give me ten minutes.
note: 약속 시간에 늦을 때 문자로 가장 많이 쓰는 문장

example: Oh, my bad. I thought the meeting was at 3.
example_ko: 아, 내 잘못이야. 회의가 3시인 줄 알았어.
note: 가벼운 실수에만 사용, 정중한 사과는 아님

example: You look exhausted. Take it easy this weekend.
example_ko: 너 완전 지쳐 보여. 주말엔 좀 쉬어.

example: Hang on a sec, I can't find my keys.
I'm really into K-dramas these days.
example_ko: 나 요즘 한국 드라마에 완전 빠졌어.

example: Meet at seven? — Sounds good!
note: 제안에 동의할 때 가장 무난한 대답

example: I'm on my way. See you in five.
I have a bunch of things to do today.
Are you moving to Seoul? For real?
Hit me up when you're free.
Let's catch up over coffee soon.
example_ko: 조만간 커피 마시면서 밀린 얘기 하자.

example: I can't wait for the concert!
I have some stuff to take care of.
Are you done? — Yeah, pretty much.
I know it's tough, but hang in there.
example_ko: 힘든 거 알아, 그래도 조금만 버텨.

example: Sorry I'm late! — No worries.
I can't figure out how to use this app.
note: 일상 회화 최고 빈출 구동사 중 하나

example: Why don't you come over for dinner?
Can you grab my phone from the table?
I was about to call you!
Can you help me move? — You bet!
I haven't seen him in a while.
example_ko: 그를 못 본 지 꽤 됐어.

example: It turned out he was right.
Guess what? I got the job!
I'll send it to you right away.
It looks hard, but I'll give it a try.
note: give it a shot도 같은 뜻

example: What are you up to tonight?
note: What are you doing?의 캐주얼 버전

example: That movie was amazing! — Totally.
He didn't show up to the party.
Sorry, I can't make it tomorrow.
note: 약속 취소할 때 가장 흔한 문장

example: How about Friday at six? — That works.
I'm tired, so I'll just stay in tonight.
example_ko: 피곤해서 오늘 밤엔 그냥 집에 있을래.

example: Check out this video. It's hilarious.
example_ko: 이 영상 좀 봐. 완전 웃겨.

example: Let's eat. I'm starving!
Can I use your charger? — Sure, go ahead.
example_ko: 충전기 좀 써도 돼? — 응, 써.

example: I can't go shopping. I'm broke this month.
example_ko: 쇼핑 못 가. 이번 달에 완전 거지야.

example: Don't hang up! I have one more thing to say.
example_ko: 끊지 마! 할 말이 하나 더 있어.

meaning: 데리러 가다, 전화를 받다, 사 오다
example: Can you pick me up at the station?
example_ko: 역으로 나 데리러 와줄 수 있어?
note: 상황 따라 뜻이 여러 개인 만능 구동사

example: You're not coming? How come?
Dinner is on me tonight.
Everything will work out fine.
note: 문맥에 따라 두 가지 뜻 모두 초빈출

example: It's been raining three days in a row.
What did you say? — Never mind.
Are you free this Saturday?
Sorry, something came up. Can we reschedule?
example_ko: 미안, 갑자기 일이 생겼어. 다시 잡아도 될까?

example: I lost my wallet. — Oh no, that sucks.
I'm looking for a new apartment.
Grab a seat. The show is about to start.
You cook, I'll do the dishes. — Deal!
example_ko: 네가 요리하면 설거지는 내가 할게. — 콜!

example: I'm exhausted after work today.
Can I take a rain check on dinner?
Hurry up! We're going to miss the bus.
She's really good at cooking.
I want to apply for that job. — Go for it!
By the way, did you finish the report?
Can you speak a little bit slower?
Oh, now I get it!
Can I try on these shoes?
No cake for me. I'm on a diet.
example_ko: 난 케이크 안 먹을래. 다이어트 중이야.

example: Can you give me a ride to the airport?
You know Jenny too? What a small world!
example_ko: 너도 제니 알아? 세상 참 좁다!

example: It was great meeting you. Let's keep in touch!
The hike was hard, but the view was worth it.
example_ko: 등산은 힘들었지만 경치가 그만한 가치가 있었어.
note: Is it worth it?(그럴 만해?)도 자주 씀

example: I'm busy right now. Can I call you back?
example_ko: 지금 좀 바빠. 다시 전화해도 될까?

example: Let's eat out tonight. I don't feel like cooking.
I feel like watching a movie tonight.
example_ko: 오늘 밤엔 영화 보고 싶은 기분이야.
note: 부정형 I don't feel like it도 초빈출

example: Hey! Long time no see. How have you been?
note: 뒤에 How have you been?이 세트

example: Do you get along with your coworkers?
We're running out of time!
Hold up, you two are dating?
The test came back negative. — That's a relief!
I'm looking forward to seeing you.
I'll stop by the store on my way home.
example_ko: 집에 가는 길에 가게에 잠깐 들를게.
note: drop by, swing by도 같은 뜻

example: Are you still up? It's 2 a.m.!
I'll be there in a couple of minutes.
There's food in the fridge. Help yourself.
We got lost on the way to the hotel.
I'm in the mood for something spicy.
How's the new job? — So far, so good.
Put on your jacket. It's cold outside.
You made it! I thought you couldn't come.
We're leaving for the airport at six.
I eat fast food once in a while.
note: every once in a while로도 씀

example: Chinese or pizza? — It's up to you.
What time do you get off work?
She can't talk now. She's on the phone.
example_ko: 걔 지금 통화 중이라 얘기 못 해.

example: Come on, we don't have all day!
Can you take out the trash?
note: 식당에서 to go와 takeout 둘 다 포장

meaning: 거의 다 왔다 / 거의 다 됐다
example: Keep going, we're almost there!
note: 물리적 거리와 진행 상황 모두에 씀

example: I have no idea where my phone is.
example_ko: 내 폰이 어디 있는지 전혀 모르겠어.

example: It's my treat today. Order anything!
Call me as soon as you get home.
Just mix and bake. That's it!
example_ko: 섞어서 굽기만 하면 돼. 그게 다야!
